lines.text: Office Memorandum • UNITED STATES GOVERNMENT
TO : Director, FBI
DATE: August 27, 1947
FROM : SAC, Butte
SUBJECT: FLYING DISCS SIGHTED BY [REDACTED] b7c
TACOMA, WASHINGTON
SECURITY MATTER - X
Re Seattle tels August 7, 1947, and August 12, 1947, in the above
captioned matter.
[REDACTED] Mountain View Drive, Boise, Idaho, was inter-
viewed at the Boise Resident Agency, Boise, Idaho, by SA [REDACTED]
August 19, 1947. Mr. [REDACTED] advised that he received a letter from [REDACTED]
[REDACTED] Editor, [REDACTED] Evanston, Illinois, dated June 26, 1947, in which
Mr. [REDACTED] advised that he was interested in publishing an article in his magazine
concerning the flying discs seen by Mr. [REDACTED] near Mount Rainier on June 24, 1947,
which letter is being enclosed to the Bureau. Mr. [REDACTED] stated that he did not
give much thought to [REDACTED] letter until [REDACTED] wrote him again and informed
him that [REDACTED] of Tacoma, Washington, had sent him b7c
fragments of a flying disc and that he would like him [REDACTED] to go to Tacoma,
Washington, and contact [REDACTED] to find out if there was any truth in
their story about the disc fragments. He stated that he did not answer [REDACTED]
letter, but inquired of several friends for their opinion in the matter, and
that they all told him if [REDACTED] desired to pay his expenses to go to Tacoma,
Washington, and look into this matter, that they did not see where he had anything
to lose. He informed that he gave a talk at the Boise Ad Club on July 25, 1947,
about the flying discs he had seen, and also of the request contained in [REDACTED]
letter to go to Tacoma to investigate additional information regarding the find-
ing of disc fragments by [REDACTED]
[REDACTED] advised that following his talk, he was approached by a person
who said he knew both [REDACTED] and that they were very reliable
individuals. He stated that after thinking it over, he called [REDACTED] by telephone
and at which time he informed [REDACTED] that he would contact [REDACTED] and
investigate the matter regarding the disc fragments, if [REDACTED] would send him
$200.00 expense money. He also advised that he received $200.00 expense money b7c
from [REDACTED] via Western Union the next morning. He informed that he left for
Tacoma, Washington, via his personal plane on July 30, 1947. Upon arriving at
Pendleton, Oregon, he told his story to [REDACTED], CAA Inspector from Portland,
Oregon, who advised him to go ahead with his investigation.
He stated that he landed at Barry's Airport in Tacoma and obtained a
room at the Winthrop Hotel. He informed that he then called [REDACTED] by telephone to
make arrangements to talk to him regarding the disc fragments. He advised that
[REDACTED] insisted on coming to the hotel room immediately. Upon arriving at Mr. [REDACTED]
hotel room, [REDACTED] stated that he wanted to forget about the discs as too many things
had been happening to him, and advised Mr. [REDACTED] to return home and forget about
the discs. Mr. [REDACTED] stated that he informed [REDACTED] that he wanted to obtain all
COPIES DESTROYED
b7c
